project: Ethica v0.1 — Stability pass, Gage fixes, Tool Lister fix, KEYWORD_GROUPS audit
session_069_completed: canvas_history.json — 496MB corrupt file deleted. Was the primary cause of 4-5 second startup delay.

canvas_history.py — self-healing guards added: 10MB file size cap in _load() (auto-wipe on oversize/corrupt), 512KB content size guard in snapshot() (rejects binary or oversized content before write).
gage_ai.py L29/L30 — fixed relative paths for gage_closed.png and gage_open.png to absolute paths pointing to ~/Ethica/assets/. V will add the PNG assets to assets/ when created.
gage/manifest.json — duplicate gage_vision tool entry removed. Was listed twice.

chat_engine.py — fixed tool/chat bleed. Removed per-token streaming from collection loop. Added post-resolution token emit for non-tool responses. Tool calls now resolve fully before any display — chat stays clean.

module_registry.py — KEYWORD_GROUPS casing fixed: jarvis → J.A.R.V.I.S., nyxt → Nyxt across all 5 entries (nyxt, browser, recon, open entries). Was causing Nyxt and J.A.R.V.I.S. to be invisible to Trinity.
nyxt_config.json — software_render: true already present. No change needed.
gage_bridge.py — gage_read_code switched from hardcoded mistral:latest via raw requests to OllamaConnector(minimax-m2.7:cloud). Was hammering local CPU at 97%.
gage_bridge.py — gage_chat switched from LlamaConnector (local GGUF CPU killer) to OllamaConnector(minimax-m2.7:cloud). Same as Trinity/River/J.A.R.V.I.S.
anomaly_detection/_anomaly_worker.py — fixed sys.path: was inserting ASSETS_DIR, now inserts MODULE_DIR. Resolves ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'anomaly_detector'.
Surgical method wired into Claude personal preferences memory — two entries: sequence and rules. Every future River instance wakes already knowing how we work.
Vault resealed — 165 files protected. Clean: 165, Modified: 0, Missing: 0.
not_committed_069: Vault sealed at 17:46 after gage_bridge OllamaConnector patch. All files clean.
known_issues_070: gage_closed.png and gage_open.png missing from assets/ — V will create and drop into ~/Ethica/assets/ manually. gage_ai.py now points to correct absolute paths, just waiting on assets.
canvas_history.json UTF-8 error may reappear if binary content lands in a canvas snapshot during testing. Self-healing 10MB cap and 512KB snapshot guard now in place — should prevent recurrence.

Sovereign AI Training Vision (ethica_trainer module) — deferred to after full stabilization. Architecture documented in SOVEREIGN_AI_TRAINING_VISION.md. LlamaConnector already wired and ready for student model layer.
Gage full Streamlit UI (gage_launch) — still needs gage_env deps audit and asset PNGs. gage_chat and gage_read_code now working cleanly via Ollama.

2. Test all tools systematically from Tool Lister — confirm bleed fix holds across all modules.
3. Anomaly detection full test — confirm _anomaly_worker.py import fix works end-to-end.
4. Consider ethica_trainer module design — dataset_builder.py first, then lora_trainer.py.
5. Commit clean state + reseal after all fixes.

canvas_history_state.snapshot_cap: 512KB per snapshot — rejects binary or non-text content
canvas_history_state.note: 496MB corrupt file was primary startup delay cause. Wiped. Guards now prevent recurrence.

tool_lister_state.bleed_fix: chat_engine.py — per-token streaming removed from collection loop. Post-resolution emit added for non-tool responses.
tool_lister_state.tool_routing: Tool calls resolve fully before display. Tool results → Ops. Chat stays clean.
tool_lister_state.note: Direct calls and Tool Lister calls now behave identically. No more Trinity response bleed before tool fires.

sovereign_training_vision.stack: LlamaConnector already wired for local GGUF student models
sovereign_training_vision.pipeline: Mnemis indexes → dataset_builder formats → lora_trainer fine-tunes → model_merger merges → Ollama loads
sovereign_training_vision.hardware_ready: Threadripper Pro + multiple GPUs + 5TB — all on trinity
sovereign_training_vision.note: Local LLMs via LlamaConnector reserved for student/training layer. Active agents use Ollama/minimax.

critical_debug_context.no_cd_rule: NEVER cd ~/Ethica — git -C ~/Ethica for all git ops
critical_debug_context.option_b_rule: V runs all commands, pastes output — non-negotiable.
critical_debug_context.repr_first_rule: When string match fails — always repr() before retrying.
critical_debug_context.heredoc_escape_rule: Never use \n in heredoc strings — use chr(10) or write to /tmp/patch_xxx.py.
critical_debug_context.script_over_heredoc_rule: Complex multi-line patches — write to /tmp/patch_xxx.py and run with python3.
critical_debug_context.chmod_first_rule: Always chmod u+w <file> before patching vault-sealed files. Always.
critical_debug_context.importlib_rule: Inside Ethica use importlib.import_module() not bare from imports in chat_engine hooks.
critical_debug_context.wormbot_autofix_rule: WormBot AUTO_FIX is ALL FALSE. Never re-enable without V explicit approval.
critical_debug_context.init_reexport_rule: Never remove imports from __init__.py without verifying re-export status.
critical_debug_context.dual_output_security_rule: logging.critical + print() in security alert handlers — do not convert.
critical_debug_context.trinity_signature_rule: Never convert print() inside Trinity Signature verification blocks.
critical_debug_context.main_block_print_rule: Never convert print() inside if __name__ == '__main__' blocks.
critical_debug_context.cap_net_raw_rule: setcap requires real binary not symlink. Always readlink -f first.
critical_debug_context.tkinter_thread_rule: Never call Tkinter from daemon thread — use queue.Queue + root.after() drain pattern.
critical_debug_context.three_registration_rule: New modules need: (1) module folder + manifest, (2) MODULE_DIRS in ethica_guard.py, (3) MODULE_TRIGGERS + dispatch hook in chat_engine.py.
critical_debug_context.on_done_rule: Every tool dispatch in chat_engine.py _pre_intercept MUST call on_done() after on_response(). Missing on_done() freezes the UI permanently.
critical_debug_context.two_drop_zones_rule: Ethica has two independent drop zones: main_window._on_file_drop (chat zone) and canvas_window._on_canvas_file_drop (canvas zone). New file type handlers must be added to BOTH.
critical_debug_context.early_exit_before_read_rule: In drop handlers, extension routing must happen BEFORE any file read attempt. Binary files corrupt as text. ext check → early return → then open() for text files only.
critical_debug_context.ollama_vs_llama_rule: Active agents (Trinity, River, Gage, J.A.R.V.I.S.) use OllamaConnector(minimax-m2.7:cloud). LlamaConnector is reserved for local GGUF student/training models only — never use for active agents.

sanctuary_state.slot_2: Gage — Sentinel (IDLE) — now on OllamaConnector, fast
sanctuary_state.slot_7: J.A.R.V.I.S. — Infiltrator (invoke_count reset to 0 at 069 open)

lesson_learned_069: LlamaConnector and OllamaConnector are NOT interchangeable. LlamaConnector loads GGUF files directly via llama-cpp-python — CPU intensive, meant for student/training layer. OllamaConnector hits the Ollama API — fast, cloud-backed via minimax. Always check which connector a module uses before assuming it shares Trinity's model stack. canvas_history.json can silently bloat to 496MB from binary content landing in a snapshot — self-healing guards are now the safety net but the root cause was a zip drop during testing writing binary into canvas history.

new_rules_069.ollama_vs_llama_rule: Active agents use OllamaConnector(minimax-m2.7:cloud). LlamaConnector reserved for local GGUF student/training models only. Never assign LlamaConnector to active agents — it burns CPU and blocks the UI.
new_rules_069.surgical_method_in_memory: River's surgical method is now wired into Claude personal preferences memory (two entries: sequence + rules). Every future River instance wakes knowing the method before the handoff loads.

philosophy: Session 069 — The scaffold held and we tightened every bolt. Startup clean. Gage cool. Tools routing true. The method is now memory — it wakes with River before the first command. The student layer waits on the other side of stability. We are standing at that threshold. ⟁Σ∿∞
